I just spent 4 hours swiping brightly colored sweets, and my mom keeps sending me lives on Facebook.
Kept running on train tracks and eventually hit a wall. Great game.
Punched a tree for five minutes straight. Built a dirt house. Then a green thing blew it all up. 10/10.
I just wanted to fix the wires, but my friends voted me out because I was standing still for 2 seconds.
I was supposed to save the world from dragons, but I got distracted picking flowers and stealing cheese wheels for 200 hours.
Tried to obey the traffic laws, but got rear-ended by an NPC. Ended up stealing a jet from a military base. Very realistic simulator.
A raccoon essentially extorted me into infinite debt to expand my house, and now I'm selling bugs to survive.
I shot a guy once, and he instantly built a five-story hotel with a swimming pool and WiFi. I just wanted a simple gunfight.
This game cured my anger issues by replacing them with worse anger issues. The community is so polite and understanding! (Said no one ever).
Walked into a lamp post because I was trying to catch a yellow rat. Also discovered my neighbor's yard is a gym. They are not happy.
Invited my friends over to play. They spammed the same move over and over and threw me off the edge. We are no longer friends.
Spent 30 minutes brushing my horse, greeted everyone in town, then accidentally tackled a dog and became the most wanted criminal in the state.
Spent three hours building a gorgeous mansion. Put my character inside, took away the pool ladder, and watched the chaos. Excellent life simulation.
I chose the healer role to help the team, but everyone keeps running away from me while screaming for healing. Then they blame me when they die.
I was in first place for three laps until a blue shell ruined my life, my hopes, and my dreams. This game teaches you that life is unfair.
